name: Foursquare Places
what_it_does: Places search and point-of-interest (POI) data API with global coverage of 100M+ places, venue details, categories, photos, tips, and location intelligence for geospatial applications.
use_cases: Nearby places search — finding restaurants, shops, or services around a coordinate
Place enrichment — given an address, retrieving structured business category and attribute data
Location-based recommendations in travel or local discovery agent workflows
Verifying and standardizing place names and categories in data pipelines
Foot traffic patterns and visit trends for location intelligence (enterprise tier)
not_for: Turn-by-turn navigation or routing (use Google Maps or TomTom Routing API)
Geocoding addresses to coordinates (use Google Geocoding or OpenCage)
Free access at production scale — meaningful usage requires paid plan
best_when: You need high-quality, global POI data with rich attributes (hours, photos, categories) and a developer-friendly places search API for location-aware agent features.
avoid_when: Your primary need is geocoding (address-to-coordinates) rather than place search, or you need free access at scale.

auth.notes: API key in Authorization header. Key scoped to account with rate limits based on plan. Formerly had OAuth for user-specific check-in data — that API is retired.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 1,000 API calls/day for Places Search and Details
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth from $199/month; Enterprise with custom pricing for foot traffic and movement data
pricing.notes: Free tier is useful for prototyping. Production use requires paid plans. Enterprise location intelligence (movement trends, catchment areas) is significantly more expensive.

performance.rate_limits: Free: 1,000 calls/day; paid plans with higher daily and per-minute limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard REST API with consistent error format. Rate limit errors include X-RateLimit headers. Place not found returns 404 clearly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All operations are read-only GET requests — fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: User check-in API and social features from the original Foursquare are retired — only Places API remains
Place categories use Foursquare's own taxonomy (not Google's) — mapping to other systems requires translation
Search results return Foursquare Place IDs which differ from Google Place IDs — not interchangeable
Photos and tips data is community-sourced and may be outdated for less popular venues
Global coverage varies significantly — US and EU have much better coverage than other regions
Free tier daily limit resets at midnight UTC — build monitoring for limit exhaustion

security_score_components.security_notes: API key auth. Places data — location intelligence. GDPR for location data handling. User check-in data involves PII. SOC2 not prominent.
